rag_rules: Fix unresolved identifiers like 'autopilot_soft' by ensuring they are visible or properly declared in the same annex scope.
Do not add new architecture elements when resolving validator errors; focus only on repairing existing constructs.
generic_rules: Ensure all identifiers referenced in AGREE annexes are visible within the same scope or explicitly declared.
Place assumptions and guarantees in appropriate component type or implementation scopes based on their port and feature references.
Use only named constants or visible features when defining range constraints in component contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses must reference valid expressions with known types; avoid using undefined identifiers.
Range checks in AGREE can use helper boolean variables defined within the same annex block.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing new components or features to resolve AGREE validation errors; restrict repairs to existing architecture.
Do not assume that missing identifiers are implicitly available; always verify visibility in the current scope.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
